channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 13 states to sue over DOGE access to government payment systems containing personal data
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in several states vowed Thursday to file a lawsuit to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing federal payment systems containing Americans' sensitive personal information.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah Legislature bans collective bargaining for teachers unions and other public sector jobs
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Labor unions that serve teachers, firefighters, police and other public employees in Utah will no longer be able to negotiate on behalf of their workers under a bill that received final legislative approval Thursday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration sues Chicago in latest crackdown on 'sanctuary' cities
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Trump administration sued Chicago on Thursday alleging that 'sanctuary' laws in the nation's third-largest city “thwart" federal efforts to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: Genesee County Chris Swanson has told The Associated Press he's launching a Democratic bid to be Michigan's next governor.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Public objections stall State Library defunding bill
item.description: “I did receive a lot of letters and the outpouring from the hearts of the little children were really, really special to me,” Rep. Lana Greenfield said.
item.dc:creator: Gracie Terrall, NewsNation

item.title: Daytona boss inspires students with his NASCAR journey
item.description: The "Super Bowl" of racing, the Daytona 500, will be the next biggest event in the world of sports, taking place February 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Davis Suppes, NewsNation

item.title: Willie Nelson mural demolished in downtown Austin
item.description: A nearly decade-old Willie Nelson mural is being torn down in Austin as part of a demolition project.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE officials hone in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Crayola brings back 8 retired colors: Here's what they are
item.description: Crayola is going back to the drawing board with its latest collection, bringing back a special assortment of retired colors for the first time in its 122-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA, NTSB to brief senators on Washington, DC midair collision
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration and National Transportation Safety Board are planning to brief senators just one week after a midair collision claimed the lives of 67 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican troops begin arriving at US-Mexico border following deal made to pause Trump-approved tariffs
item.description: Mexico has started deploying soldiers to its northern border in accordance with the 10,000 troops it promised to send to the border under a deal made with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama puts man to death for a 1991 murder in the nation's fourth execution using nitrogen gas
item.description: Alabama has carried out the nation’s fourth nitrogen gas execution, putting a man to death for the killing of a woman who was raped and shot during an apartment break-in.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The CIA used unclassified email to share a list of employees hired within the last two years to the Office of Personnel Management (OPM).
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump is targeting antisemitism in schools. Experts fear other civil rights will be ignored
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal office that enforces civil rights at schools across the U.S. has been ordered to prioritize complaints of antisemitism above all else as it molds to President Donald Trump’s agenda, raising fears that other rights violations will go unpunished.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New NASA imagery shows a large asteroid could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrats accuse DOGE of going after NOAA
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday accused Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) of improperly inserting itself into the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). In a joint statement, Reps. Zoe Lofgren (D-Calif.) and Jared Huffman (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to sign executive order barring transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KTLA 5

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: McConnell slips and falls outside Senate Chamber
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) slipped and fell on the stairs leaving the Senate Chamber on Wednesday afternoon, prompting concerned aides to rush to help the 82-year-old lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma board denies clemency for man on death row, clears path for March execution
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma board rejected clemency on Wednesday for a man sentenced to die for fatally shooting a woman during a 2005 home-invasion robbery, clearing the way for him to become the first person executed in the state this year.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service flip-flops on Hong Kong-China packages, lifting a ban imposed a day earlier
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is reversing course a day after placing a ban on all inbound packages from China and Hong Kong. The post office had announced Tuesday that it would no longer accept parcels from the China and Hong Kong after the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in California
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Still locked out of federal funding, several Head Start preschools may need to close temporarily
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start preschools serving thousands of children around the country could be at risk of closing because they remain locked out of their federal funding, a problem that first surfaced last week during President Donald Trump's aborted effort to freeze federal grants.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, Fox 5
